albums.title: Amnesiac
albums.reason: With 2050 all-time plays, Radiohead is your second most-played artist ever, and Amnesiac is the darker, more experimental twin to Kid A — a record you probably haven't revisited properly in years.

albums.title: Alive 2007
albums.reason: Daft Punk sits at the very top of your all-time chart with 2940 plays, and this live record captures the exact peak-era energy behind Veridis Quo, Digital Love, and Aerodynamic — all in your top tracks.

albums.title: The Dark Side of the Moon
albums.reason: Pink Floyd has 1199 all-time plays and you've loved Animals and Wish You Were Here deeply — The Dark Side of the Moon is the eternal centrepiece of that catalogue and worth a full immersive spin.

albums.title: 10 000 Hz Legend
albums.reason: Air sits at 1011 all-time plays and Sexy Boy and La femme d'argent are deep in your top tracks — this weirder, less-visited second album is a perfect companion to Moon Safari you may not have touched in a decade.

albums.title: Room on Fire
albums.reason: The Strokes have 652 all-time plays and Barely Legal has appeared in your liked tracks — Room on Fire is the scrappier, more intense follow-up to Is This It and absolutely deserves a revival.

albums.title: Blue Lines
albums.reason: Massive Attack has 534 all-time plays and Mezzanine has been in your rotation — Blue Lines is where it all began, a foundational record you may not have spun in full for years.

albums.title: James Blake
albums.reason: James Blake sits at 637 all-time plays and CMYK has 90 plays in your top tracks — his self-titled debut is where that haunting, post-dubstep minimalism was fully realised, and it was a big record for you in the early 2010s.

albums.title: The North Borders
albums.reason: Bonobo has 427 all-time plays and Kiara is a liked track — The North Borders is his most emotionally expansive record and sits perfectly in the 2013 era of your listening history.

albums.title: Lost
albums.reason: Trentemøller has 560 all-time plays and Into the Trees and Take Me Into Your Skin have both appeared in your history — Lost is his most song-driven, darkly cinematic album and likely peaked in your rotation in the mid-2010s.

albums.title: The Inevitable End
albums.reason: Röyksopp has 1062 all-time plays and The Girl and the Robot is deep in your top tracks — The Inevitable End was their farewell to the album format, released as a poignant 2014 closer that you may not have revisited since.

albums.title: III
albums.reason: Rusty Nails has 66 plays in your all-time top tracks and Moderat II has been in your past recommendations — III is their most polished and emotionally restrained record, from a period you were clearly deep in.

albums.title: There Is Love in You
albums.reason: Four Tet has 34 plays in your last 12 months and New Energy has been in recent rotation — There Is Love in You is his most beloved, euphoric record and likely a touchstone from the early 2010s you haven't fully revisited.

albums.title: Evenfall
albums.reason: Your love of Air, Röyksopp, and Daft Punk points to a deep affection for French-inflected, melodic electronic music — Schuller sits in that exact space with a more introspective, late-night quality you'd likely love.

albums.title: Précis
albums.reason: Your all-time love of Aphex Twin, Plaid, and the more ambient end of electronic music makes Pioulard a natural step — he sits at the intersection of shoegaze and IDM with a warmth that mirrors your deep listening habits.

albums.title: Prins Thomas
albums.reason: Your all-time obsession with Röyksopp and the Norwegian electronic scene makes Prins Thomas an obvious but overlooked thread — this debut is a masterpiece of slow, cosmic disco that feels like a natural sibling to Melody A.M.

albums.title: From Here We Go Sublime
albums.reason: Your love of Underworld, Orbital, and deep-running electronic repetition points directly to The Field — this debut is one of the most critically adored minimal techno records of the 2000s and belongs in your collection.

albums.title: Plans Drawn in Pencil
albums.reason: Your deep history with Plaid, Aphex Twin, and the Warp Records universe makes Isan a perfect discovery — their melodic, gentle IDM sits in the exact emotional register of your most-played artists without any of the same recognition.

albums.title: Kid Kin
albums.reason: Your love of Modeselektor, James Blake, and the more leftfield end of electronic music makes Kidkanevil a compelling find — Kid Kin blends hip-hop texture with abstract electronic warmth in a way that mirrors your all-time taste perfectly.

albums.title: A Certain Distance
albums.reason: Your love of Trentemøller, Plaid, and the more melodic side of electronic music points directly to Lusine — Two Dots appeared in your previous picks already as a song, meaning you're already circling this artist, and A Certain Distance is his finest album.

albums.title: To Cy & Lee: Instrumentals Vol. 1
albums.reason: Your recent immersion in Nils Frahm, Hania Rani, and contemplative piano music aligns perfectly with this record — it's humble, warm, and unhurried in exactly the way your current listening arc suggests you need.

albums.title: Fugitive Light and Themes of Consolation
albums.reason: Your recent plays of Brambles, A Winged Victory for the Sullen, and Ólafur Arnalds suggest a hunger for slow, cinematic emotional music — Wasylyk sits in that exact space with a distinctly Scottish pastoral quality.

albums.title: Femenine
albums.reason: Your recent arc through Nils Frahm, Ben Lukas Boysen, and Martin Kohlstedt points toward a love of minimalist, evolving structures — Eastman's Femenine is a radical, deeply moving work that sits at the outer edge of that world.

albums.title: Palmbomen II
albums.reason: Your recent listening to Khruangbin, St Germain, and Nightmares on Wax reveals a love of blissed-out, groove-driven texture — Palmbomen II sits right in that lane with a woozy, slow-funk warmth that feels made for your current mood.

albums.title: Mango
albums.reason: Your recent love of Super Flu, Folamour, and DJ Koze points toward a taste for underground, texture-rich dance music with soul — Khidja's Mango is a cult Romanian house gem that lives in exactly that space.

albums.title: Dandelion
albums.reason: Your recent listening to Brambles, Hania Rani, and Ólafur Arnalds suggests a thread toward quiet, emotionally fragile music — Tara Nome Doyle sits at the intersection of Nordic folk and chamber minimalism in a way that feels perfectly timed.

albums.title: The Universe Smiles Upon You II
albums.reason: Khruangbin has absolutely dominated your last 7 days with 34 plays, and Little Joe and Mary ii appeared in your most recent tracks — this is the natural next spin in that same warm, Texan groove universe.

albums.title: Sonido Cósmico
albums.reason: Hermanos Gutiérrez sit at 17 plays this week and Until We Meet Again and El Bueno Y El Malo are both in your most recent tracks — Sonido Cósmico is the deeper dive into that cinematic, guitar-led tranquillity you're clearly craving right now.

albums.title: Living Fields
albums.reason: Portico Quartet are at 17 plays this week and Ruins is one of your most-played recent tracks — Living Fields is their most spacious and emotionally open record, a step beyond the self-titled album you've been deep in.

albums.title: Everything
albums.reason: Ben Lukas Boysen is at 5 plays this week and appears consistently across your recent listening — Everything is his most orchestrally rich record and sits perfectly in the contemplative, late-evening mood you're in right now.

albums.title: Found Songs
albums.reason: Ólafur Arnalds is at 5 plays this month and 21:05 is in your recent tracks — Found Songs is his most intimate and stripped-back work, perfectly matched to the quiet, inward mood of your current week.

albums.title: The Local Police
albums.reason: Arc De Soleil appeared at 8 plays this week and The Local Police itself is in your most recent tracks — this is the album you're actively discovering right now and deserves a proper full listen.

songs.title: Pyramid Song
songs.reason: With Reckoner at 125 plays and True Love Waits in your all-time tops, you clearly love Radiohead's most emotionally devastated moments — Pyramid Song is the peak of Amnesiac and a track that deserves to surface again.

songs.title: Teardrop
songs.reason: Massive Attack has 534 all-time plays and Mezzanine has been deep in your history — Teardrop is the centrepiece of that record and one of the most emotionally striking tracks in your entire taste universe.

songs.title: Halcyon + On + On
songs.reason: Orbital have 777 all-time plays and Don't Stop Me and The Box are both in your history — Halcyon is the track that made them legends and it's been long enough that it deserves to feel brand new again.

songs.title: The Sunshine Underground
songs.reason: The Chemical Brothers sit at 556 all-time plays in your history and this deep album cut from Surrender captures the peak of their late-90s psychedelic power — a track from the era you clearly returned to most.

songs.title: Stairway to Heaven
songs.reason: Led Zeppelin have 964 all-time plays and Kashmir and The Rain Song are both in your track history — Stairway to Heaven is the cathedral of the catalogue and a track worth revisiting as pure deep nostalgia.

songs.title: Moaner
songs.reason: Underworld have 633 all-time plays and Born Slippy and Cowgirl are both in your history — Moaner is the bruising, nine-minute deep cut from Second Toughest that rewards a proper late-night revisit.

songs.title: Kong
songs.reason: Bonobo has 427 all-time plays and Kiara is a liked track — Kong is the propulsive standout from Migration and captures the more dancefloor-leaning side of his sound that defined that era of your listening.

songs.title: Tongue
songs.reason: Maribou State have 433 all-time plays and Beginner's Luck is at 65 plays in your top tracks — Tongue is a deep favourite from Kingdoms in Colour and a track from the era when you were clearly playing them heavily.

songs.title: Reminder
songs.reason: Rusty Nails has 66 all-time plays and Moderat III sits in the recent vault era — Reminder is the emotional high point of that album, a track that felt enormous in 2016 and will hit just as hard now.

songs.title: Angel Echoes
songs.reason: Four Tet has 34 plays in your last 12 months and New Energy has been a recent album — Angel Echoes from There Is Love in You is the track that crystallised his sound in the early 2010s and belongs in your recent vault.

songs.title: Limit to Your Love
songs.reason: James Blake is your top all-time track artist with Postpone at 156 plays and CMYK at 90 — Limit to Your Love is the most arresting track from his debut and a perfect companion to the sounds you loved in the early 2010s.

songs.title: Breathe This Air
songs.reason: Jon Hopkins was recommended in your previous picks via Immunity and Open Eye Signal appeared as a song — Breathe This Air is the emotive, full-throttle centrepiece of that album and a track from the era you were clearly deep in.

songs.title: Release Spirit
songs.reason: Your all-time love of Boards of Canada, Aphex Twin, and the more hazy, introspective end of electronic music makes Khotin a natural discovery — Release Spirit is a perfectly drifting, washed-out gem from that universe.

songs.title: Stick to My Side
songs.reason: Your deep love of Orbital, Trentemøller, and atmospheric electronic minimalism points directly to Pantha du Prince — Stick to My Side is the luminous standout from Black Noise, a record that lives in that exact emotional space.

songs.title: Lovers' Carvings
songs.reason: Your all-time love of Bonobo, Plaid, and the more melodic, textured side of electronic music makes Bibio a natural fit — Lovers' Carvings is his most beloved track and sits beautifully adjacent to your all-time taste.

songs.title: Awake
songs.reason: Your love of Moby, Bonobo, and warmly melodic electronic music points straight to Tycho — Awake is an absolutely gorgeous standout from his best album and fills a space in your taste universe that no one else quite occupies.

songs.title: Go Hawaii
songs.reason: Your deep love of Plaid, Isan, and the Warp-adjacent world of melodic IDM makes Casino Versus Japan a perfect find — Go Hawaii is a gorgeous, underheard track from that era that feels like a missing piece in your collection.

songs.title: Approach
songs.reason: Your love of Trentemøller, Moderat, and the darker, more atmospheric end of electronic music makes Subheim a compelling discovery — Approach is a beautifully constructed slow-burner that sits right at the edge of your all-time taste.

songs.title: What You Get For Being Alive
songs.reason: Your recent love of DJ Koze, Super Flu, and blissed-out, slow-motion electronic music points directly to Suzanne Kraft — this track is a cult classic in that world and should feel immediately right.

songs.title: Nerve
songs.reason: Your recent listening to Khruangbin, Sault, and Portico Quartet suggests a love of sophisticated, groove-driven soul — Jordan Rakei sits in that exact space and Nerve is a standout from his finest album.

songs.title: In Circles
songs.reason: Your recent immersion in Nils Frahm, Joep Beving, and Hania Rani makes this a natural step — Reinhardt Vanbergen occupies the same quiet, piano-led contemplative space and In Circles is an understated gem.

songs.title: Love Is the Message
songs.reason: Your recent arc through Portico Quartet, Makaya McCraven, and Sault reveals a love of jazz-influenced, rhythmically rich music — Love Is the Message is Yussef Dayes at his most euphoric and it would fit perfectly in your current rotation.

songs.title: Falling
songs.reason: Your recent love of Weval, Apparat, and melodic, emotionally open electronic music makes Parra for Cuva a perfect find — Falling is a delicate, enduring track that sits right in the sweet spot of your current listening arc.

songs.title: Some Other Day
songs.reason: Your recent plays of Joep Beving, Martin Kohlstedt, and Ben Lukas Boysen reveal a love of understated, emotionally resonant piano music — Mooryc sits in that exact lane with a gentle, human quality that feels very much of the moment.

songs.title: Cómo Te Quiero
songs.reason: Khruangbin are dominating your week with 34 plays and Cómo Te Quiero appeared in your most recent track history — this is the deep, swaying groove you keep returning to right now.

songs.title: El Camino Eternal
songs.reason: Hermanos Gutiérrez are at 17 plays this week and you've been deep in El Bueno Y El Malo and Sonido Cósmico — El Camino Eternal is a transportive, unhurried desert guitar piece that fits the mood you're in perfectly.

songs.title: Lullaby for Humans
songs.reason: Portico Quartet are at 17 plays this week and Ruins has been on heavy rotation — Lullaby for Humans is a quieter, more intimate gem from their later catalogue that suits the contemplative quality of your current listening.

songs.title: Pygmalion
songs.reason: Ben Lukas Boysen has 5 plays this week and Gravity and Spells have both been in recent rotation — Pygmalion is a swelling, cinematic standout from Everything that captures the emotional scale you seem to be reaching for right now.

songs.title: You
songs.reason: Nils Frahm is at 12 plays this month and My Friend The Forest and Says are both in your recent tracks — You is a tender, unhurried piece from Solo that fits the quiet, introspective mood of your current week.

songs.title: Particles
songs.reason: Ólafur Arnalds has 5 plays this month and 21:05 is in your recent track history — Particles is a luminous, emotionally open piece from re:member that perfectly captures where your head seems to be this week.

Daphni - FOLD, London. Nov 22, 2025. 7hr DJ set
